description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, and test gaps in the Plugin SDK browser-context utilities discovered during a comprehensive audit. All changes are internal to platform/plugin-sdk/ — no browser-observable behavior changes.

userStories.title: Handle invalid CSS selectors gracefully in DOM utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want DOM utilities to throw clear SDK errors when I pass an invalid CSS selector so that I get a descriptive message instead of a cryptic DOMException.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForSelector with an invalid selector (e.g., '[invalid') rejects with an Error whose message includes 'invalid selector' or 'invalid CSS' — not a raw DOMException
waitForSelectorRemoval with an invalid selector rejects with a descriptive Error
querySelectorAll with an invalid selector returns an empty array instead of throwing
getTextContent with an invalid selector returns null instead of throwing
observeDOM with an invalid selector throws a descriptive Error (existing behavior is already descriptive for missing elements, extend to invalid selectors)
New unit tests cover invalid selector input for all five DOM utilities
Existing tests still pass — no behavioral change for valid selectors
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts

The issue: document.querySelector() throws DOMException when given an invalid CSS selector string. Currently, waitForSelector (line 37), waitForSelectorRemoval (line 78), querySelectorAll (line 112), getTextContent (line 120), and observeDOM (line 135) all call querySelector/querySelectorAll without catching DOMException.

For waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval: the querySelector call is inside a Promise constructor, so the DOMException will reject the promise — but the error message is the browser's cryptic DOMException rather than a clear SDK message. Wrap the initial querySelector in try-catch and re-throw with a descriptive message like: `waitForSelector: invalid CSS selector "${selector}": ${error.message}`.

For querySelectorAll: wrap in try-catch, return empty array on DOMException (consistent with 'no match' behavior).

For getTextContent: wrap in try-catch, return null on DOMException (consistent with 'no match' behavior).

For observeDOM: the existing error on line 137 already throws for 'no element found'. Add a try-catch around querySelector to produce a similar message for invalid selectors: `observeDOM: invalid CSS selector "${selector}": ${error.message}`.

Also add a test in dom.test.ts for needsAttributeObservation — use a class selector like waitForSelector('.my-class', { timeout: 100 }) to verify the observer gets attributes: true. This is a separate test gap but belongs in the same file.

Test file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.test.ts

userStories.title: Fix getPageGlobal to traverse function-type intermediates
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want getPageGlobal to traverse function objects with properties so that I can access paths like 'Array.isArray' or framework singletons that are callable.
acceptanceCriteria: getPageGlobal('Array.isArray') returns the Array.isArray function (not undefined)
getPageGlobal traverses through function-type intermediate values that have properties
getPageGlobal still returns undefined for primitive intermediates (number, string, boolean) — no regression
getPageGlobal still returns undefined for null/undefined intermediates — no regression
New unit tests cover: function-type intermediate with properties, callable global with nested property
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.ts, line 17

Current code:
```
if (current === null || current === undefined || typeof current !== 'object') return undefined;
```

This check rejects functions because `typeof function === 'function'`, not 'object'. But functions in JS are objects that can have properties (e.g., `Array.isArray`, `Object.keys`, or framework singletons like `window.React.createElement`).

Fix: change the condition to also allow functions:
```
if (current == null || (typeof current !== 'object' && typeof current !== 'function')) return undefined;
```

Note: `current == null` covers both null and undefined in one check.

Test file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.test.ts
Add tests:
1. Set globalThis.__testGlobal to a function with a property: `const fn = () => {}; fn.version = '1.0'; globalThis.__testGlobal = fn;` then verify `getPageGlobal('__testGlobal.version')` returns '1.0'.
2. Verify `getPageGlobal('Array.isArray')` returns a function (since Array is a function with isArray property).

userStories.title: Add DOM node handling to log serializer JSON replacer
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want logging objects that contain DOM node references to serialize quickly and informatively so that log calls don't hang while traversing large DOM subtrees.
acceptanceCriteria: Logging an object like { el: document.body, name: 'test' } serializes el as a descriptive string (e.g., '[BODY]') and preserves name as 'test' — does not attempt to recursively serialize the DOM node's properties
The JSON replacer in safeSerializeArg checks for DOM nodes (nodeType property) before falling through to the generic JSON.stringify path
New unit test: log.info('test', { el: mockDomNode, name: 'hello' }) produces data with el as a descriptive string
New unit test: log.info('test', { fn: someFunction }) produces data with fn as '[Function: name]'
New unit test: log.info('test', { sym: Symbol('x') }) produces data with sym as '[Symbol: x]'
Existing safe serialization tests still pass
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add AbortSignal support to waitUntil
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want waitUntil to accept an AbortSignal so that polling stops immediately when a tool invocation is cancelled, instead of continuing until the full timeout expires.
acceptanceCriteria: WaitUntilOptions interface accepts an optional signal: AbortSignal property
waitUntil rejects immediately with the abort reason when the signal is already aborted before calling
waitUntil rejects with the abort reason when the signal fires during polling, cleaning up the timeout timer and polling timer
waitUntil still works identically when no signal is provided — no regression
New unit test: signal already aborted rejects immediately
New unit test: signal aborted during polling stops polling and rejects
New unit test: retry's abortableSleep path — signal aborted between retries during the sleep delay
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add test for fetchJSON with content-length 0 on 200 response
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the fetchJSON content-length: 0 code path to be covered by a test so that changes to the empty-body handling logic are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: New test: fetchJSON returns undefined for a 200 response with content-length: 0 and no body (when no schema is provided)
New test: fetchJSON throws ToolError.validation for a 200 response with content-length: 0 when a schema is provided
Test HTTP server has a new /empty-200 endpoint that responds with 200 and content-length: 0
